summary: Hand-authored `module` command (of/create-issue/sync) plus a withModuleEnrichment wrapper that auto-enriches issue module_ids at the tail of `sync`. Includes dogfood hardening: `module of` validates the issue UUID and ensures the junction table exists; `module sync` reports a zero-result no-op (exit 0) on an empty cache.
reason: Plane's issue serializer never returns module membership and issueCreate has no module field, so a plain sync leaves module_ids null. The enrichment walks module-issues into a module_issues junction table and patches issues.data.module_ids; create-issue does create + add-to-module in one call. The UUID guard / table-ensure / empty-cache-no-op keep the stateful commands safe to invoke before any sync (and pass the Phase 5 live dogfood matrix).
validated_outcome: Live dogfood matrix passes (251 pass / 0 fail). Module enrichment fires from the wrapped sync (PLANE_SLUG-sourced): module_membership_synced modules 17, links 384, issues_patched 383 against the baseline workspace; the junction table is created at runtime so no store.go schema patch is needed.
